title: moquiniastrum gardneri
intro: Moquiniastrum gardneri, also known as the 'Gardner's Moquiniastrum', is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, native to Argentina and Uruguay. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea, and as an anti-inflammatory agent to alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. The health benefits of Moquiniastrum gardneri include its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and infections. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve its leaves, which can be made into teas or infusions, or dried and used in capsules or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
